last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

moves.applicability: This tactic is already concrete for the current goal.
moves.why_relevant: The proof candidate is closed; run verification next.

cut_position.tactic: seq 1 1 : (c2{1} = c1{2} /\ n{1} = n{2} /\ a{1} = a{2} /\ p0{1} = p0{2} /\ p{1}.`1 = n{1} /\ p{2}.`1 = n{2} /\ Mem.log{1} = Mem.log{2} /\ Mem.lc{1} = Mem.lc{2} /\ BNR.lenc{1} = BNR.lenc{2} /\ BNR.ndec{1} = BNR.ndec{2} /\ !(n{1} \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} => nn \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} => nn \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => nn \in n{1} :: BNR.lenc{1})).

selected_handles.why_relevant: The proof candidate is closed; run verification next.

proof_piece_memory.interpretation: This is manager-owned evidence about prior route repairs and daemon-verified route options. Replayable pieces should still be verifier-checked before commit; stale and negative pieces are warnings, not tactics.

lineage_briefing.interpretation: Manager-owned continuity notes for this node. Use them as resume/navigation evidence; EasyCrypt replay still decides whether any old route piece is valid.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: The proof candidate is closed; run verification next.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.

items.label: Restore before last checkpoint rewind
items.why_checkpoint: state saved before the last checkpoint rewind; selecting it returns to that proof state
items.effect_if_selected: This restores the branch to the proof state saved before the last checkpoint rewind.

items.label: Before latest branch-local tactic #26
items.why_checkpoint: latest branch-local step inside the current seq scope
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #26 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before latest call-obligation tactic #25
items.why_checkpoint: call-obligation local boundary; selecting it keeps the opened call obligation and removes later local work
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #25 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After seq opened / before branch work #18
items.committed_tactic: exlim n{2}, SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1}, SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} => n0 mr0 ms0; call (equ_cc n0 mr0 ms0).
items.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #18 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: After call opened / before obligation work #10
items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #10 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before call invariant #9
items.committed_tactic: call (_: inv_cpa SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} Mem.log{1} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{1} Mem.lc{2} BNR.lenc{1} BNR.lenc{2} BNR.ndec{1} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (forall n c, (n, c) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => n \in BNR.lenc{1})).
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #9 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before seq cut #17
items.committed_tactic: seq 1 1 : (c2{1} = c1{2} /\ n{1} = n{2} /\ a{1} = a{2} /\ p0{1} = p0{2} /\ p{1}.`1 = n{1} /\ p{2}.`1 = n{2} /\ Mem.log{1} = Mem.log{2} /\ Mem.lc{1} = Mem.lc{2} /\ BNR.lenc{1} = BNR.lenc{2} /\ BNR.ndec{1} = BNR.ndec{2} /\ !(n{1} \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitC2.I1.RO.m{1} => nn \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitC2.I2.RO.m{1} => nn \in BNR.lenc{1}) /\ (forall nn ci, (nn, ci) \in SplitD.ROF.RO.m{1} => nn \in n{1} :: BNR.lenc{1})).
items.why_checkpoint: seq-cut boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this cut was committed
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #17 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before inline expansion #24
items.committed_tactic: proc; inline*; auto; rewrite /inv_cpa /=; smt(get_setE mem_set).
items.why_checkpoint: inline boundary; selecting it restores the proof state before this expansion
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #24 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
